evidence_quotes.quote: end its support, so the product does not work anymore at all.

paragraphs.text: DFA should in my opinion also accommodate for Stop Killing Games initiative, as a current trend in the industry changes the balance and traditional expectation of digital products working as long as user has hardware capable of running the software. This has been changing recently and currently many software developers make a product in a way, that they can and do end its support, so the product does not work anymore at all. I would not expect an indefinite product support for single payment, that would not be fair. But end of support does not have to mean end of software package as a whole. For example Windows XP have lost support a long time ago, but if you bought it and need it for your application, you can still run it. Requirement for this behaviour should be a part of DFA. Thank you.
